title: 6. Bayesian VAR and DSEM
text: estimators expect long format: one row per person-occasion, id column, numeric time-varying indicators ordered within person. bundled srl data hold self-regulated-learning indicators 36 students measured 156 occasions . single-person calls fit one student, Grace, five indicators; multilevel calls use full panel 36 students variables. estimators absorb assumption violations silently — trend, instance, inflates autoregressive diagonal rather producing error — stationarity screen precedes fit. Grace’s 156 ordered occasions yield 155 complete current/lagged pairs, series trips trend, high-autoregression, drift, unit-root, zero-variance flag, models specified series stand.

title: 6. Bayesian VAR and DSEM
text: single-person posterior medians reproduce ordinary VAR pattern. Monitoring occasion t−1t-1 predicts lower value occasion tt (posterior median −0.158), planning predicts higher effort (0.159), largest contemporaneous edge monitoring–effort (0.467). None temporal credible intervals excludes zero, medians descriptive rather evidence selected temporal paths. accessors serve point-estimate fits apply: summary() reports one row per network layer, edges() lists edges decreasing magnitude, nodes() gives node-level strength, matrices() returns underlying posterior-median matrices. Bayesian mlVAR fit gives temporal mean absolute weight 0.012, contemporaneous mean absolute weight 0.165, -person mean absolute weight 0.206 panel. Monitoring occasion t−1t-1 effort occasion tt largest average temporal edge (posterior median 0.032). Planning–effort largest contemporaneous edge (0.274), efficacy–planning largest -person edge (0.558) — statement students report high efficacy high planning average, either process unfolding within student. plot() method draws layers conventions estimators: arrows lag-one prediction temporal panel, undirected edges contemporaneous layers, width scaled absolute weight colour encoding sign.        evaluated calls remove gap displayed code reported output, still examples rather universal MCMC prescription. Applied analyses require problem-specific chain lengths, convergence diagnostics, posterior predictive checks, sensitivity analyses priors. fit_mlvar_mplus() additionally depends external Mplus installation, demonstrated call template.

title: 10. Clean-room design
text: clean-room implementation reproduce statistical estimand without copying external package’s internals. idiographic, means explicit lag construction, standardized accessors, shared print plot conventions, tests reference behaviour possible. package separates temporal, contemporaneous, -person layers interpretations differ (Epskamp et al. 2018). accessor contract deliberately small. summary() reports network-level density signed-edge counts. edges() returns one row per edge. coefs() includes full coefficient cells available. nodes() summarizes strength, -strength, -strength, self-loops. matrices() returns estimator matrices. common surface makes estimator differences visible without erasing .

title: 10. Clean-room design
text: Ordinary VAR unregularized single-person baseline (Bringmann et al. 2013). Graphical VAR adds LASSO graphical-lasso regularization EBIC model selection. mlVAR estimates average within-person temporal contemporaneous layers plus -person network (Epskamp et al. 2018). Bayesian VAR DSEM add posterior uncertainty Mplus-oriented dynamic SEM links. uSEM GIMME use SEM path vocabularies, GIMME searching group individual paths.

title: 10. Clean-room design
text: srl input can audited passed multiple estimators common interface. audit reports 5548 retained lagged pairs unit-root zero-variance flags. Grace used none five series receives audit flag; selection based input diagnostics, fitted network looks best. common preprocessing surface estimator vignettes. two summaries expose design contrast. OLS reports full temporal contemporaneous density mean absolute weights 0.075 0.160. Graphical VAR reports zero temporal edges three contemporaneous edges. accessor shape makes regularization consequence explicit. ordinary VAR edge table contains small lagged effects monitoring later value (−0.160) planning later effort (0.159). graphical VAR retains contemporaneous monitoring–effort, efficacy–monitoring, planning–effort edges. temporal edge -> means occasion t−1t-1 predicts occasion tt; contemporaneous graphical VAR edge undirected partial correlation.

Mplus-backed functions require licensed software file-based workflows. uSEM GIMME depend SEM convergence search behaviour. Stability forecast helpers experimental diagnostics without single canonical reference implementation.

title: 10. Clean-room design
text: Uniform accessors imply identical estimands. GIMME prevalence edge, mlVAR fixed effect, graphical VAR partial correlation, forecast residual answer different questions. Clean-room validation therefore read layer layer estimator estimator, primary literature defining target reproduction.

title: 8. GIMME
text: estimator expects long format: one row per person-occasion, id column, ordering column, numeric time-varying indicators. supplied anonymized esm_srl data hold momentary self-regulated-learning indicators 41 students; name identifies student occasion orders measurements within student. Five indicators enter model: efficacy, value, planning, monitoring, effort. keep vignette fast without hand-picking people fitted networks, example uses eight students complete five-indicator occasions, breaking ties alphabetically. transparent rule selects Hana, Iker, Jamal, Omar, Quinn, Yara, Cira, Enzo: six 79 complete occasions two 78. audit shown rather silently assuming stationarity; supplied series contain trend shift flags, fit method demonstration paths treated confirmatory substantive findings.
code: vars <- c("efficacy", "value", "planning", "monitoring", "effort") complete_n <- tapply(complete.cases(esm_srl[vars]), esm_srl$name, sum) selection <- data.frame(subject = names(complete_n),                         complete = as.integer(complete_n)) selection <- selection[order(-selection$complete, selection$subject), ] selected_ids <- head(selection$subject, 8) head(selection, 8) #>    subject complete #> 15    Hana       79 #> 17    Iker       79 #> 19   Jamal       79 #> 29    Omar       79 #> 32   Quinn       79 #> 40    Yara       79 #> 5     Cira       78 #> 10    Enzo       78 preprocess(esm_srl[esm_srl$name %in% selected_ids, ],            vars = vars, id = "name") #> Idiographic Preprocessing #>   Variables:      5 (efficacy, value, planning, monitoring, effort) #>   Ordered rows:   630 #>   Retained pairs: 622 #>   Trend flags:    21 #>   High AR flags:  0 #>   Drift flags:    12 #>   Unit-root risk: 0 #>   Zero variance:  0 #>   Tables:         x$pairs | x$counts | x$diagnostics #>  #> 21 of 40 subject-series show a trend or unit-root that can bias the temporal network. preprocess() only diagnosed this; to clean just the series that need it, re-run with: #>   preprocess(data = esm_srl[esm_srl$name %in% selected_ids, ], vars = vars, id = "name", detrend = "auto")

title: 8. GIMME
text: estimator takes data, variable set, id column, time column; time = "occasion" orders occasions within student. ar = TRUE places autoregressive path every variable every subject’s model outset, anchors search. groupcutoff = 0.75 subcutoff = 0.75 require path improve fit least 75 percent relevant subjects — , six eight — promoted corresponding rule. cross-variable path reaches 75 percent group threshold. five autoregressive paths fixed ar = TRUE carried eight students; selected cross-variable paths individual-level. result stated rule cutoffs, claim population shared dynamics. fitted temporal contemporaneous prevalence matrices range 0.125 (one student) 0.50 (four students) diagonal.
code: students <- esm_srl[esm_srl$name %in% selected_ids, ] gimme_fit <- fit_gimme(   students, vars = vars, id = "name", time = "occasion",   ar = TRUE, groupcutoff = 0.75, subcutoff = 0.75, seed = 1 ) gimme_fit #> GIMME Network Analysis #> ------------------------------  #> Subjects:   8  #> Variables:  5  ( efficacy, value, planning, monitoring, effort ) #> AR paths:   yes  #> Hybrid:     no  #>  #> Group-level paths found: 0  #>  #> Individual-level paths:  mean 5.1, range 2-9 #>  #> Proportion of subjects with each path: #>  #>   Temporal [directed] #>     weights [0.125, 1.000]  |  +13 / -0 edges #>                efficacy value planning monitoring effort #>     efficacy       1.00  0.00     0.25       0.12   0.00 #>     value          0.25  1.00     0.00       0.00   0.12 #>     planning       0.00  0.12     1.00       0.12   0.00 #>     monitoring     0.00  0.00     0.25       1.00   0.00 #>     effort         0.00  0.12     0.00       0.00   1.00 #>  #>   Contemporaneous [directed] #>     weights [0.125, 0.500]  |  +13 / -0 edges #>                efficacy value planning monitoring effort #>     efficacy       0.00  0.00     0.00       0.00   0.12 #>     value          0.38  0.00     0.38       0.12   0.50 #>     planning       0.38  0.00     0.00       0.00   0.50 #>     monitoring     0.25  0.12     0.25       0.00   0.00 #>     effort         0.12  0.00     0.25       0.38   0.00 #>  #>   plot(x)  (faithful gimme-style mixed network) | plot(x, layer = "temporal")  #>   edges(x) | nodes(x) | summary(x) | coefs(x) | matrices(x)

title: 8. GIMME

title: 9. Rolling networks
text: Plotting rolling fit fit = 1 draws selected window’s temporal contemporaneous panels side side, edge width scaled absolute weight colour encoding sign.  first-window OLS plot dense, every unregularized window : 25 possible temporal arrows 10 possible contemporaneous edges carries estimate, eye drawn width rather presence.  temporal panel isolates local lag-one structure — monitoring--efficacy planning--effort effects read table .  contemporaneous panel carries efficacy–monitoring–effort core also anchors Grace’s full-series fits.  sparse window keeps core sets remaining within-occasion associations exact zeros. Redrawing panel four windows, varying fit, graphical form question rolling estimation answers: whether person’s local structure holds steady moves protocol.
code: plot(rolling_ols, fit = 1) plot(rolling_ols, fit = 1, layer = "temporal") plot(rolling_ols, fit = 1, layer = "contemporaneous") plot(rolling_gvar, fit = 1, layer = "contemporaneous")

title: 7. Unified SEM
text: estimator takes long-format panel estimators: one row per person-occasion, id column, numeric time-varying indicators ordered within person. bundled srl data hold self-regulated-learning indicators 36 students measured 156 occasions ; vignette fits Grace five indicators: efficacy, value, planning, monitoring, effort. Grace chosen five series pass input audit, network returned later. uSEM dynamic lag-one model absorbs assumption violations silently — trending series inflates lagged coefficients rather producing error — stationarity screen precedes fit. Grace’s 156 rows yield 155 complete lagged pairs. None five series trips trend, high-autoregression, mean-shift, variance-shift, unit-root, zero-variance screen, series fitted supplied.

what: Idiographic Person-Specific and Heterogeneous Complex Networks
title: Idiographic Person-Specific and Heterogeneous Complex Networks
text: Network estimation intensive longitudinal data — person-specific within-person temporal, contemporaneous, -subject networks ESM / EMA / diary panels, one tidy verb per method. idiographic estimates dynamic networks intensive longitudinal data (ILD): ordinary regularized vector autoregression, multilevel VAR, native Bayesian multilevel VAR validated selected Mplus DSEM fixtures, unified SEM, GIMME — plus supporting workflow (preprocessing audits, edge-stability diagnostics, rolling windows, forecast validation, model comparison, idiographic supervised machine-learning models individualized prediction). Every result tidy .data.frame() summary() views. Network estimates additionally share edges(), nodes(), coefs(), matrices(), plot(), as_netobject().

title: Idiographic Person-Specific and Heterogeneous Complex Networks
text: core estimators native R implementations published modelling targets, consistent interface validation reference outputs reference implementation available: CRAN package offline-first: imports standard R packages stats, utils, parallel, ship R. mandatory third-party package dependency. lme4 lavaan optional engines multilevel frequentist VAR SEM/GIMME respectively; plotting licensed Mplus bridge optional . Competitor packages 20-panel oracle corpus live repository’s separate validation/ lane shipped CRAN tarball. Bayesian DSEM sampler particular highlight: fit_mlvar_bayes() targets output mlVAR::mlVAR(estimator = "Mplus") — Mplus’s two-level Bayesian VAR latent mean centring — without Mplus installed, using pure-R conjugate Gibbs sampler hand-rolled inverse-Wishart draws (MCMCpack/rstan). committed evidence consists fixed bivariate Mplus fixtures, one univariate random-AR fixture, parameter-recovery tests; use equivalence(fit) inspect precise scope rather assuming blanket DSEM equivalence.

title: Idiographic Person-Specific and Heterogeneous Complex Networks
text: core can installed downloaded source tarball without network access; optional engines checked corresponding methods called. CRAN: author’s r-universe (recommended — compilation, binaries included): GitHub: Plotting uses cograph package; stays optional offered -demand install first time call plot().
code: install.packages("idiographic") install.packages("idiographic",                  repos = c("https://mohsaqr.r-universe.dev",                            "https://cloud.r-project.org")) # install.packages("pak") pak::pak("mohsaqr/idiographic")

title: Idiographic Person-Specific and Heterogeneous Complex Networks
text: fitting functions use named, readable arguments. list_estimators(), estimator_info(), get_estimator() expose registry; custom methods can added register_estimator(). equivalence_table() reports package-wide evidence status, argument_coverage() guarantees every current public formal classified oracle/engine/statistical/internal, delegated, extension, explicit rejection boundary. Together ledgers provide complete package-wide evidence closure: unassessed registered methods arguments. Numerical equivalence remains method- configuration-specific rather blanket package claim.
code: library(idiographic)  ## simulate an ESM panel: 30 people, 40 beeps, 3 items set.seed(1) panel <- do.call(rbind, lapply(1:30, function(id) {   y <- matrix(0, 40, 3)   for (t in 2:40) y[t, ] <- c(0.35, 0.30, 0.25) * y[t - 1, ] + rnorm(3)   data.frame(id = id, beep = 1:40, A = y[, 1], B = y[, 2], C = y[, 3]) }))  ## multilevel VAR: temporal, contemporaneous, and between networks fit <- fit_mlvar(panel, vars = c("A", "B", "C"), id = "id", beep = "beep")  fit                 # tidy printout of all three networks edges(fit)          # one row per edge (network, from, to, weight) coefs(fit)          # fixed-effect estimates with SE / p / CI plot(fit)           # draw all layers with cograph plot(fit, layer = "temporal")  ## the same call through the registry-driven front door fit2 <- fit_idiographic(   panel, method = "mlvar",   params = list(vars = c("A", "B", "C"), id = "id", beep = "beep") ) equivalence(fit2)  # exact validation scope and tolerance declaration  ## inspect the complete package and argument-by-argument evidence ledgers equivalence_table() argument_coverage("mlvar")

title: Idiographic Person-Specific and Heterogeneous Complex Networks
code: bayes <- fit_mlvar_bayes(panel, vars = c("A", "B", "C"),                            id = "id", beep = "beep",                            n_iter = 4000, n_chains = 2) bayes               # posterior medians, SDs, 95% CIs, convergence (max PSR) coefs(bayes)  ## full DSEM with person-specific slopes, random residuals, and ## within-model imputation of missing observations (needs enough subjects to ## identify the random-effect covariance: at least 2 * (p + p^2) + 1): fit_mlvar_bayes(panel, vars = c("A", "B", "C"), id = "id", beep = "beep",                   temporal = "random", residual = "random", impute = TRUE)

title: Idiographic Person-Specific and Heterogeneous Complex Networks
text: Estimators fit_var() / fit_var_each() — ordinary VAR(1) (OLS), pooled per subject fit_graphical_var() / fit_graphical_var_each() — regularized graphical VAR (GLASSO + EBIC), including explicit multi-lag layers fit_mlvar() — frequentist multilevel VAR fixed, correlated, orthogonal, unique person-specific temporal/contemporaneous structures fit_mlvar_bayes() — native Bayesian multilevel VAR / DSEM (fixed random slopes, fixed random residual covariance, optional within-model imputation) fit_var_bayes() — native Bayesian VAR(1) fit_mlvar_mplus() — true-Mplus backend (wraps mlVAR(estimator = "Mplus")) fit_usem() — unified Structural Equation Modeling (lavaan) fit_gimme() — Group Iterative Multiple Model Estimation explicit Bonferroni/FDR corrections, alpha, stopping criteria fit_ml() — individualized supervised prediction models, comparing person-specific models pooled baseline held-within-person rows, new dependencies Workflow & diagnostics preprocess() — preprocessing audit ILD (compliance, variance, stationarity) estimate_stability() — bootstrap edge-stability diagnostics (experimental) fit_rolling_var() / fit_rolling_graphical_var() — rolling-window (time-varying) networks validate_forecast() — rolling --sample forecast validation (experimental) compare_idiographic() — model-comparison reports Tidy contract Every result: .data.frame() · summary() · print() Network results: edges() · nodes() · coefs() · matrices() · plot() / plot_gimme() · as_netobject()

title: Idiographic Person-Specific and Heterogeneous Complex Networks
text: Use model = "" run native models selected task. regression includes mean baseline, OLS (linear), ridge, lasso, elastic net, PCR, kNN, one-split tree. binary classification includes majority baseline, logistic regression, ridge/lasso/elastic-net logistic, LDA, Gaussian naive Bayes, kNN, one-split tree. Use estimator = "native" explicitly want pin implementation; future package backends live behind model name.
code: ml <- fit_ml(   panel,   outcome = "A",   predictors = c("B", "C"),   id = "id",   beep = "beep",   compare = "both",   model = c("linear", "ridge", "knn") )  ml                 # per-person and pooled held-out performance ml$metrics         # MAE / RMSE / bias / R-squared by subject and overall coefs(ml)          # coefficients for each individualized and pooled model ml$predictions     # row-level held-out predictions

code: W <- matrix(c(0, 0.3, -0.2, 0), 2, 2,             dimnames = list(c("A", "B"), c("A", "B"))) x <- structure(list(weights = W, method = "relative", directed = TRUE),                class = "cograph_network") as_netobject(x) #> $data #> NULL #>  #> $weights #>     A    B #> A 0.0 -0.2 #> B 0.3  0.0 #>  #> $nodes #>   id label name  x  y #> 1  1     A    A NA NA #> 2  2     B    B NA NA #>  #> $edges #>   from to weight #> 1    2  1    0.3 #> 2    1  2   -0.2 #>  #> $directed #> [1] TRUE #>  #> $method #> [1] "relative" #>  #> $params #> list() #>  #> $scaling #> NULL #>  #> $threshold #> [1] 0 #>  #> $n_nodes #> [1] 2 #>  #> $n_edges #> [1] 2 #>  #> $level #> NULL #>  #> $meta #> $meta$source #> [1] "idiographic" #>  #> $meta$layout #> NULL #>  #> $meta$tna #> $meta$tna$method #> [1] "relative" #>  #>  #>  #> $node_groups #> NULL #>  #> attr(,"class") #> [1] "cograph_network" "netobject"       "list"

what: Plottable netobject(s) from a GIMME fit — as_netobject.net_gimme
title: Plottable netobject(s) from a GIMME fit — as_netobject.net_gimme
text: Returns GIMME result matrix-backed netobjects. default encode quantity gimme package plots — proportion subjects path (path_counts / n_subjects) — group-average coefficient (dilutes toward zero GIMME displays). faithful single mixed network (dashed lag / solid contemporaneous, group/individual colouring, autoregressive self-loops) use plot_gimme().

title: Plottable netobject(s) from a GIMME fit — as_netobject.net_gimme

title: Plottable netobject(s) from a GIMME fit — as_netobject.net_gimme
text: x net_gimme object. style Either "pnode" (default) — netobject_group two directed p-node networks, $temporal (lagged; autoregression diagonal) $contemporaneous (-beep), matching shape fit_graphical_var() returns — "unified", single directed 2p-node network *_lag half feeding current half (literal uSEM topology). weight Either "prop" (default) — edge weight proportion subjects path — "coef", group-average standardized coefficient. ... Unused.

title: Plottable netobject(s) from a GIMME fit — as_netobject.net_gimme
text: style = "pnode", netobject_group $temporal $contemporaneous. style = "unified", one c("netobject", "cograph_network") object 2p nodes.

title: Tidy coefficients from a fitted mlvar model — coefs.idioml_result
code: # \donttest{ set.seed(1) n_id <- 8; n_t <- 30; vars <- c("A", "B", "C") rows <- lapply(seq_len(n_id), function(i) {   m <- as.data.frame(matrix(rnorm(n_t * 3), ncol = 3))   names(m) <- vars   m$id <- i; m$day <- 1L; m$beep <- seq_len(n_t)   m }) d <- do.call(rbind, rows) fit <- fit_mlvar(d, vars = vars, id = "id", day = "day", beep = "beep") #> Warning: Model for 'A': singular fit (random-effects variance near zero). #> Warning: Model for 'A': boundary (singular) fit: see help('isSingular') #> Warning: Model for 'B': singular fit (random-effects variance near zero). #> Warning: Model for 'B': boundary (singular) fit: see help('isSingular') #> Warning: Model for 'C': singular fit (random-effects variance near zero). #> Warning: Model for 'C': boundary (singular) fit: see help('isSingular') #> Warning: Between-subjects network not estimable: a random-intercept SD is 0 (no between-person variance). Returning a zero matrix by convention (mlVAR returns NA here). print(fit) #> mlVAR result: 8 subjects, 232 observations, 3 variables (lags 1) #>   Temporal edges significant at p<0.05: 1 / 9 #>  #>   Temporal [directed] #>     weights [-0.131, 0.062]  |  +3 / -6 edges #>           A     B     C #>     A -0.13 -0.05 -0.03 #>     B -0.01 -0.08 -0.01 #>     C  0.06  0.02  0.04 #>  #>   Contemporaneous [undirected] #>     weights [0.006, 0.085]  |  +3 / -0 edges #>          A    B    C #>     A 0.00 0.06 0.08 #>     B 0.06 0.00 0.01 #>     C 0.08 0.01 0.00 #>  #>   Between [undirected] #>     no non-zero edges #>       A B C #>     A 0 0 0 #>     B 0 0 0 #>     C 0 0 0 #>  #>   plot(x) | plot(x, layer = "temporal") | plot(x, layer = "between")  #>   edges(x) | nodes(x) | summary(x) | coefs(x) | matrices(x) summary(fit) #>           network n_nodes n_edges density mean_abs_weight n_positive n_negative #> 1        temporal       3       6       1      0.03140474          2          4 #> 2 contemporaneous       3       3       1      0.05004674          3          0 #> 3         between       3       0       0      0.00000000          0          0 # }

what: Compare idiographic estimators on one dataset — compare_idiographic
title: Compare idiographic estimators on one dataset — compare_idiographic
text: Fits one idiographic estimators data returns tidy per-method/per-network comparison table. reporting layer: define new model, row computed estimator's summary() method plus common edge-table accessors.

title: Compare idiographic estimators on one dataset — compare_idiographic
code: compare_idiographic(   data,   vars,   estimators = c("var", "graphical_var"),   id = NULL,   day = NULL,   beep = NULL,   estimator_args = list(),   keep_fits = FALSE )

title: Compare idiographic estimators on one dataset — compare_idiographic
text: data data.frame matrix columns variables optional id/day/beep columns. vars Character vector variable names. estimators Character vector naming registered network estimators fit. Built-values "var", "var_bayes", "graphical_var", "mlvar", "mlvar_bayes", "mlvar_mplus", "usem", "gimme". id Character. Name person-ID column, NULL. day Character. Name day/session column, NULL. beep Character. Name measurement-occasion column, NULL. estimator_args Named list per-estimator argument lists, e.g. list(graphical_var = list(n_lambda = 8), usem = list(temporal = "ar")). keep_fits Logical. Store fitted model objects? Default FALSE.

title: Compare idiographic estimators on one dataset — compare_idiographic
text: model_comparison object $comparison, $failures, optionally $fits. $comparison tidy data.frame one row per method/network.

title: Compare idiographic estimators on one dataset — compare_idiographic
code: set.seed(1) d <- data.frame(id = 1, day = rep(1:4, each = 15),                 beep = rep(1:15, 4),                 A = rnorm(60), B = rnorm(60), C = rnorm(60)) cmp <- compare_idiographic(   d, vars = c("A", "B", "C"), id = "id", day = "day", beep = "beep",   estimators = c("var", "graphical_var"),   estimator_args = list(graphical_var = list(n_lambda = 5)) ) cmp$comparison #>          method         network n_nodes n_edges density mean_abs_weight #> 1           var        temporal       3       6       1      0.09014205 #> 2           var contemporaneous       3       3       1      0.08260114 #> 3 graphical_var        temporal       3       0       0      0.00000000 #> 4 graphical_var contemporaneous       3       0       0      0.00000000 #>   n_positive n_negative n_self max_abs_weight #> 1          3          3      3      0.1899219 #> 2          2          1      0      0.1304348 #> 3          0          0      0      0.0000000 #> 4          0          0      0      0.0000000

code: # S3 method for class 'net_usem' edges(   x,   sort_by = "weight",   include_self = FALSE,   network = NULL,   n = NULL,   ... )  # S3 method for class 'var_result' edges(   x,   sort_by = "weight",   include_self = FALSE,   network = NULL,   n = NULL,   ... )  edges(x, ...)  # S3 method for class 'netobject' edges(   x,   sort_by = "weight",   include_self = FALSE,   network = NULL,   n = NULL,   ... )  # S3 method for class 'netobject_group' edges(   x,   sort_by = "weight",   include_self = FALSE,   network = NULL,   n = NULL,   ... )  # S3 method for class 'gvar_result' edges(   x,   sort_by = "weight",   include_self = FALSE,   network = NULL,   n = NULL,   ... )  # S3 method for class 'net_mlvar' edges(   x,   sort_by = "weight",   include_self = FALSE,   network = NULL,   n = NULL,   ... )  # S3 method for class 'net_gimme' edges(   x,   sort_by = "weight",   include_self = TRUE,   weight = c("prop", "coef"),   network = NULL,   n = NULL,   ... )  # S3 method for class 'var_list' edges(x, ...)  # S3 method for class 'gvar_list' edges(x, ...)

title: Tidy edge table for any idiographic result — edges.net_usem
text: x gvar_result, net_mlvar, net_gimme, netobject, netobject_group. sort_by "weight" (descending |weight|) NULL natural order. include_self Keep autoregressive self-loops? Default FALSE (TRUE GIMME, autoregression point). network Optional character vector selecting network layer(s) return (e.g. "temporal", "contemporaneous", ""). Default NULL returns every layer. unknown layer errors lists available ones. n Optional integer. Keep first n edges, strongest absolute weight sort_by = "weight". Default NULL returns edges. ... Passed methods. weight GIMME : "prop" (proportion subjects, default) "coef" (group-average coefficient) edge weight.

title: Report method-equivalence evidence — equivalence
code: set.seed(2) d <- data.frame(A = rnorm(40), B = rnorm(40)) fit <- fit_var(d, vars = c("A", "B"), scale = FALSE) equivalence(fit) #> Idiographic equivalence declaration #>   Method:    var #>   Status:    validated #>   Reference: stats::lm.fit #>   Scope:     OLS coefficient engine and package-defined VAR(1) preprocessing. #>   Tolerance: 1e-10 #>   Notes:     This is engine equivalence, not blanket equivalence to another VAR package.

what: GIMME: Group Iterative Multiple Model Estimation — fit_gimme
title: GIMME: Group Iterative Multiple Model Estimation — fit_gimme
text: Estimates person-specific directed networks intensive longitudinal data using unified Structural Equation Modeling (uSEM) framework. Implements data-driven search identifies: Group-level paths: Directed edges present majority (default 75\ Individual-level paths: Additional edges specific person, found group paths established. Uses lavaan SEM estimation modification indices. Accepts single data frame ID column (CSV directories).

title: GIMME: Group Iterative Multiple Model Estimation — fit_gimme
code: fit_gimme(   data,   vars,   id,   time = NULL,   day = NULL,   beep = NULL,   min_obs = NULL,   subject = NULL,   ar = TRUE,   standardize = FALSE,   groupcutoff = 0.75,   subcutoff = 0.75,   paths = NULL,   exogenous = NULL,   hybrid = FALSE,   VAR = FALSE,   rmsea_cutoff = 0.05,   srmr_cutoff = 0.05,   nnfi_cutoff = 0.95,   cfi_cutoff = 0.95,   n_excellent = 2L,   seed = NULL,   group_correct = "Bonferroni Group",   indiv_correct = "Bonferroni",   alpha = 0.05,   stop_crit = "model fit",   subgroup = FALSE,   outcome = NULL,   conv_vars = NULL,   mult_vars = NULL,   lv_model = NULL,   lasso_model_crit = NULL,   ms_allow = FALSE,   ordered = NULL,   dir_prop_cutoff = 0,   out = NULL,   sep = NULL,   header = NULL,   plot = FALSE,   sub_feature = "lag & contemp",   sub_method = "Walktrap",   sub_sim_thresh = "lowest",   confirm_subgroup = NULL,   conv_length = 16,   conv_interval = 1,   mean_center_mult = FALSE,   diagnos = FALSE,   ms_tol = 1e-05,   lv_estimator = "miiv",   lv_scores = "regression",   lv_miiv_scaling = "first.indicator",   lv_final_estimator = "miiv" )

title: GIMME: Group Iterative Multiple Model Estimation — fit_gimme
text: data data.frame long format columns person ID, time-varying variables, optionally time/beep column. vars Character vector variable names model. id Character string naming person-ID column. time Character string naming time/order column, NULL. provided, data sorted id time lagging. day Character string naming day/session column, NULL. supplied, lag-1 pairs formed within (id, day) block, lag never crosses overnight gap. beep Character string naming measurement-occasion column, NULL. Used (day) order observations time given. min_obs Integer NULL. Keep subjects least many observations (counts taken data). subject Optional vector naming exact subject(s) analyse. ar Logical. TRUE (default), autoregressive paths (variable predicting lag 1) included fixed paths. standardize Logical. TRUE (default FALSE), variables standardized per person estimation. Note: returned coefficient network ($coefs, $psi, $temporal_avg, $contemporaneous_avg, $group_paths) unaffected idiographic extracts standardized lavaan solution (lavInspect(fit, "std")), invariant input scaling. scale-dependent $fit statistics (chisq, aic, bic) change. groupcutoff Numeric 0 1. Proportion individuals path must significant added group level. Default 0.75. subcutoff Numeric. Subgroup cutoff (default 0.75, matching gimme); relevant subgrouping, implemented. paths Character vector lavaan-syntax paths force model (e.g., "V2~V1lag"). Default NULL. exogenous Character vector variable names treat exogenous. Default NULL. hybrid Logical. TRUE, also searches residual covariances. Default FALSE. VAR Logical. TRUE, fit standard VAR: lagged directed paths searched contemporaneous relations estimated residual covariances (directed contemporaneous paths). Matches gimme(VAR = TRUE). Default FALSE. rmsea_cutoff Numeric. RMSEA threshold excellent fit (default 0.05). srmr_cutoff Numeric. SRMR threshold excellent fit (default 0.05). nnfi_cutoff Numeric. NNFI/TLI threshold excellent fit (default 0.95). cfi_cutoff Numeric. CFI threshold excellent fit (default 0.95). n_excellent Integer. Number fit indices must excellent stop individual search. Default 2. seed Integer NULL. Random seed reproducibility. group_correct Group-level multiple-comparison correction. Use "Bonferroni Group" (default) divide alpha number people, "Bonferroni Paths" divide number eligible paths, "fdr" Benjamini-Hochberg correction, single number (0, 1) set group alpha directly. legacy misspelling "Bonferoni Group" accepted deprecation warning. indiv_correct Individual-level multiple-comparison correction. Use "Bonferroni" (default) "fdr". alpha Base significance level group individual searches. Default 0.05. stop_crit Individual-search stopping rule. "standard" stops fit adequate significant path remains; "model fit" (default) keeps adding largest-MI path, regardless significance, fit adequate; "significance" keeps adding significant paths even fit adequate. subgroup Logical. Subgrouping (S-GIMME) implemented; TRUE raises error pointing gimme::gimme(). Default FALSE. outcome, conv_vars, mult_vars, lv_model, lasso_model_crit, ms_allow, ordered, dir_prop_cutoff Accepted gimme::gimme() API parity implemented (latent variable / fMRI-convolution / multiplied-term / LASSO / ordinal / multiple-solutions / directionality features). non-default value raises error pointing gimme::gimme(). , sep, header, plot Accepted gimme::gimme() API parity. idiographic reads data.frame (CSV directory), non-default , sep, header values emit warning effect. returns object plot plot_gimme(); plot = TRUE emits message. sub_feature, sub_method, sub_sim_thresh, confirm_subgroup, conv_length, conv_interval, mean_center_mult, diagnos, ms_tol, lv_estimator, lv_scores, lv_miiv_scaling, lv_final_estimator Accepted gimme::gimme() API parity. configure unsupported subgrouping / convolution / multiplied-term / multiple-solutions / latent-variable features inert (parent feature guarded ).

title: GIMME: Group Iterative Multiple Model Estimation — fit_gimme
text: S3 object class "net_gimme" containing: temporal p x p matrix group-level temporal (lagged) path counts – entry [,j] = number individuals path j(t-1)->(t). contemporaneous p x p matrix group-level contemporaneous path counts – entry [,j] = number individuals path j(t)->(t). coefs List per-person q x (q + p) coefficient matrices (q non-exogenous rows; columns = [lagged, contemporaneous]). psi List per-person q x (q + p) standardized residual covariance matrices, non-exogenous current variables rows c(lag_names, varnames) columns, matching gimme::gimme()'s returned psi contract. fit Data frame per-person fit indices (chisq, df, pvalue, rmsea, srmr, nnfi, cfi, bic, aic, logl, status). path_counts p x 2p matrix: many individuals path. paths List per-person character vectors lavaan path syntax. group_paths Character vector group-level paths found. individual_paths List per-person character vectors individual-level paths (beyond group). syntax List per-person full lavaan syntax strings. labels Character vector variable names. n_subjects Integer. Number individuals. n_obs Integer vector. Time points per individual. config List configuration parameters.

title: GIMME: Group Iterative Multiple Model Estimation — fit_gimme
code: # \donttest{ # Create simple panel data (3 subjects, 4 variables, 50 time points). set.seed(42) n_sub <- 3; n_t <- 50; vars <- paste0("V", 1:4) rows <- lapply(seq_len(n_sub), function(i) {   d <- as.data.frame(matrix(rnorm(n_t * 4), ncol = 4))   names(d) <- vars; d$id <- i; d }) panel <- do.call(rbind, rows) res <- fit_gimme(panel, vars = vars, id = "id") print(res) #> GIMME Network Analysis #> ------------------------------  #> Subjects:   3  #> Variables:  4  ( V1, V2, V3, V4 ) #> AR paths:   yes  #> Hybrid:     no  #>  #> Group-level paths found: 0  #>  #> Individual-level paths:  mean 1.3, range 0-3 #>  #> Proportion of subjects with each path: #>  #>   Temporal [directed] #>     weights [0.333, 1.000]  |  +6 / -0 edges #>        V1 V2   V3   V4 #>     V1  1  0 0.00 0.00 #>     V2  0  1 0.33 0.33 #>     V3  0  0 1.00 0.00 #>     V4  0  0 0.00 1.00 #>  #>   Contemporaneous [directed] #>     weights [0.333, 0.333]  |  +2 / -0 edges #>        V1   V2 V3   V4 #>     V1  0 0.00  0 0.00 #>     V2  0 0.00  0 0.00 #>     V3  0 0.00  0 0.33 #>     V4  0 0.33  0 0.00 #>  #>   plot(x)  (faithful gimme-style mixed network) | plot(x, layer = "temporal")  #>   edges(x) | nodes(x) | summary(x) | coefs(x) | matrices(x) # }

title: Graphical VAR Estimation — fit_graphical_var
text: Estimate graphical vector autoregressive (GVAR) model time series panel data. Jointly estimates sparse temporal network (L1-penalized VAR coefficients) sparse contemporaneous network (graphical lasso residuals) using EBIC model selection lambda grid.

title: Graphical VAR Estimation — fit_graphical_var
code: fit_graphical_var(   data,   vars,   id = NULL,   day = NULL,   beep = NULL,   lags = 1L,   n_lambda = 50L,   gamma = 0.5,   scale = TRUE,   center_within = TRUE,   lambda_min_ratio = 0.05,   lambda_min_kappa = NULL,   lambda_min_beta = NULL,   penalize_diagonal = TRUE,   lambda_beta = NULL,   lambda_kappa = NULL,   regularize_mat_beta = NULL,   regularize_mat_kappa = NULL,   maxit_in = 100L,   maxit_out = 100L,   delete_missings = TRUE,   likelihood = c("unpenalized", "penalized"),   ebic_tol = 1e-04,   mimic = "current",   verbose = FALSE,   min_obs = NULL,   subject = NULL )

title: Graphical VAR Estimation — fit_graphical_var
text: data data.frame matrix columns variables, optionally id, day, beep columns panel/ESM data. prepared list containing numeric matrices data_c (current responses) data_l (lagged design, without intercept column) also accepted. vars Character vector variable names. May omitted prepared input data_c column names. id Character. Name person-ID column. NULL, assumes single subject. day Character. Name day/session column. Default: NULL. beep Character. Name beep/measurement column. Default: NULL. lags Positive integer vector explicit lags include. Default: 1. n_lambda Integer scalar, two-value vector giving number beta kappa penalties. latter can named, example c(beta = 30, kappa = 20), unnamed beta/kappa order. Default: 50. gamma Numeric. EBIC hyperparameter (0 = BIC, higher = sparser). Default: 0.5. scale Logical. Whether standardize variables. Default: TRUE. center_within Logical. Whether centre within person one id present (removes -person variance). Default: TRUE. lambda_min_ratio Numeric scalar, two-value beta/kappa vector. Ratio min/max lambda unless overridden per-dimension. Default: 0.05. lambda_min_kappa, lambda_min_beta Numeric NULL. Per-dimension min/max lambda ratios (matching graphicalVAR's lambda_min_kappa / lambda_min_beta). NULL, fall back lambda_min_ratio. penalize_diagonal Logical. Penalize autoregressive diagonal beta. Default: TRUE (matches graphicalVAR). lambda_beta Numeric scalar (vector), NULL. supplied, temporal penalty pinned value instead EBIC-selected grid – matching graphicalVAR's lambda_beta argument (e.g. lambda_beta = 0.1). Default NULL (EBIC grid). lambda_kappa Numeric scalar (vector), NULL. lambda_beta contemporaneous (kappa) penalty. regularize_mat_beta Optional numeric/logical matrix (p x p p x (p+1)) per-element beta penalty multipliers (matches graphicalVAR's regularize_mat_beta). NULL uses penalize_diagonal. regularize_mat_kappa Optional p x p numeric/logical matrix per-element kappa penalty multipliers (matches graphicalVAR's regularize_mat_kappa). NULL penalizes -diagonals. maxit_in, maxit_out Integer. Max inner (beta) / outer (beta-kappa) iterations. Defaults 100 (matches maxit./ maxit.). delete_missings Logical. Drop rows missing current/lagged values. Default TRUE (matches deleteMissings). likelihood Either "unpenalized" (default; refit precision EBIC, matching graphicalVAR) "penalized" (use regularized kappa directly). ebic_tol Numeric. Tolerance EBIC tie-break. Default 1e-4. mimic Character. "current" supported. Legacy modes error explicitly idiographic claim equivalence . verbose Logical. Emit progress messages. Default FALSE. min_obs Integer NULL. Keep subjects least many observations (counts taken data). Default NULL. subject Optional vector naming exact subject(s) analyse. Default NULL (subjects).

title: Graphical VAR Estimation — fit_graphical_var
text: list class gvar_result containing: beta Temporal coefficient matrix, outcome x (intercept + predictors), graphicalVAR's convention. temporal first requested p x p temporal layer [outcome, predictor]; unchanged default lag 1 fit. temporal_layers Named p x p coefficient matrices every lag. kappa Precision matrix (p x p, symmetric). PCC Partial contemporaneous correlations -cov2cor(kappa), diagonal zeroed. PDC Partial directed correlations. contemporaneous Alias PCC. labels Variable names. n_obs Number valid lag-pair observations. lambda_beta, lambda_kappa Selected penalties. gamma, EBIC EBIC gamma used selected EBIC.

title: Graphical VAR Estimation — fit_graphical_var
text: clean-room reimplementation Rothman/Epskamp two-step estimator numerically equivalent graphicalVAR::graphicalVAR(): identical data preparation (global scaling, optional within-person centring, intercept column, lag-1 construction within id/day blocks), identical lambda grids (generate_lambdas), coupled MRCE beta-update / glasso kappa-update loop, unpenalized-likelihood EBIC, tie-broken model selection. committed end--end regression tests use tolerance 1e-6, covering well-conditioned numerically difficult fits. equivalence claim limited mimic = "current" lags = 1; multiple lags idiographic extension labelled returned equivalence metadata.

title: Graphical VAR Estimation — fit_graphical_var
text: Epskamp, S., Waldorp, L. J., Mottus, R., & Borsboom, D. (2018). Gaussian Graphical Model Cross-Sectional Time-Series Data. Multivariate Behavioral Research, 53(4), 453-480. Rothman, . J., Levina, E., & Zhu, J. (2010). Sparse multivariate regression covariance estimation. JCGS, 19(4), 947-962.

title: Graphical VAR Estimation — fit_graphical_var
code: set.seed(1) d <- data.frame(A = rnorm(60), B = rnorm(60)) fit <- fit_graphical_var(d, vars = c("A", "B"), n_lambda = 3,                          scale = FALSE) fit$temporal #>   A B #> A 0 0 #> B 0 0 fit$contemporaneous #>   A B #> A 0 0 #> B 0 0

what: Fit idiographic supervised machine-learning models — fit_ml
title: Fit idiographic supervised machine-learning models — fit_ml
text: Fits train/test supervised prediction models idiographic design: subject can receive model trained subject's earlier rows, held-rows can also scored pooled model trained subjects' earlier rows. mirrors individualized modelling designs person-specific prediction compared nomothetic pooled baseline. implementation dependency-free beyond base R. Regression supports mean baseline, ordinary least squares, ridge, lasso, elastic net, principal component regression, k-nearest neighbours, one-split regression tree. Binary classification supports majority baseline, logistic regression, ridge/lasso/elastic-net logistic regression, linear discriminant analysis, Gaussian naive Bayes, k-nearest neighbours, one-split classification tree. Predictors standardized using training rows .

title: Fit idiographic supervised machine-learning models — fit_ml
code: fit_ml(   data,   outcome,   predictors,   id,   day = NULL,   beep = NULL,   task = c("auto", "regression", "classification"),   model = NULL,   estimator = NULL,   compare = c("both", "individual", "pooled"),   test_prop = 0.2,   min_train = 10L,   min_test = 1L,   lambda = 1,   alpha = 0.5,   k = 5L,   n_components = NULL,   max_iter = 100L,   tol = 1e-06,   standardize = TRUE,   keep_fits = FALSE,   ... )  fit_idiographic_ml(   data,   outcome,   predictors,   id,   day = NULL,   beep = NULL,   task = c("auto", "regression", "classification"),   model = NULL,   estimator = NULL,   compare = c("both", "individual", "pooled"),   test_prop = 0.2,   min_train = 10L,   min_test = 1L,   lambda = 1,   alpha = 0.5,   k = 5L,   n_components = NULL,   max_iter = 100L,   tol = 1e-06,   standardize = TRUE,   keep_fits = FALSE,   ... )  fit_individualized_ml(   data,   outcome,   predictors,   id,   day = NULL,   beep = NULL,   task = c("auto", "regression", "classification"),   model = NULL,   estimator = NULL,   compare = c("both", "individual", "pooled"),   test_prop = 0.2,   min_train = 10L,   min_test = 1L,   lambda = 1,   alpha = 0.5,   k = 5L,   n_components = NULL,   max_iter = 100L,   tol = 1e-06,   standardize = TRUE,   keep_fits = FALSE,   ... )

text: data data.frame matrix. outcome Character. Name outcome column. predictors Character vector predictor columns. id Character. Name subject/person ID column. day, beep Optional ordering columns. Rows ordered id, day, beep last rows subject held . task "auto", "regression", "classification". Auto treats numeric outcome regression two-level non-numeric outcome binary classification. model NULL task default, "" native models selected task, character vector simple model names. Regression models "mean", "linear", "ridge", "lasso", "elastic", "pcr", "knn", "tree". Classification models "majority", "logistic", "ridge", "lasso", "elastic", "lda", "bayes", "knn", "tree". estimator NULL model's default estimator, named character vector/list mapping model names estimator names. native base-R estimator "native". package-specific backends belong model can estimated one way. compare models fit: "" (default), "individual", "pooled". test_prop Proportion subject's ordered rows held end series. Default 0.2. min_train Minimum complete training rows required model. Default 10. min_test Minimum held-rows required per subject. Default 1. lambda Ridge penalty model = "ridge". intercept penalized. Also used lasso elastic-net models. Default 1. alpha Elastic-net mixing value [0, 1]; 0 ridge 1 lasso. Default 0.5. k Number neighbours model = "knn". Default 5. n_components Number principal components model = "pcr". Default uses min(5, n_predictors, n_train - 1). max_iter Maximum iterations coordinate-descent penalized models. Default 100. tol Convergence tolerance iterative models. Default 1e-6. standardize Logical. Standardize predictors using training-set means SDs? Default TRUE. keep_fits Logical. Store fitted internal model objects? Default FALSE. ... Optional model controls using names explicit tuning arguments (lambda, alpha, k, n_components, max_iter, tol). Unknown names rejected.

what: Build a Multilevel Vector Autoregression (mlVAR) network — fit_mlvar
title: Build a Multilevel Vector Autoregression (mlVAR) network — fit_mlvar
text: Estimates three networks ESM/EMA panel data, matching validated mlVAR::mlVAR() configurations machine precision: (1) directed temporal network fixed-effect lagged regression coefficients, (2) undirected contemporaneous network partial correlations among residuals, (3) undirected -subjects network partial correlations derived person-mean fixed effects.

title: Build a Multilevel Vector Autoregression (mlVAR) network — fit_mlvar
code: fit_mlvar(   data,   vars,   id,   day = NULL,   beep = NULL,   lags = 1L,   estimator = c("lmer", "default", "lm", "Mplus"),   temporal = c("fixed", "correlated", "orthogonal", "unique", "default"),   contemporaneous = c("fixed", "correlated", "orthogonal", "unique", "default"),   AR = FALSE,   scale = FALSE,   scaleWithin = FALSE,   nCores = 1L,   verbose = FALSE,   lag = NULL,   standardize = NULL,   min_obs = NULL,   subject = NULL,   engine = c("frequentist", "bayes", "mplus", "reference"),   standardize_mode = NULL,   missing = c("omit", "fail", "model"),   compare_to_lags = NULL,   true_means = NULL,   detrend = c("none", "position"),   na_rm = TRUE,   orthogonal = NULL,   ... )

title: Build a Multilevel Vector Autoregression (mlVAR) network — fit_mlvar
text: data data.frame containing panel data. vars Character vector variable column names model. id Character string naming person-ID column. day Character string naming day/session column, NULL. provided, lag pairs formed within day. beep Character string naming measurement-occasion column, NULL. NULL, row position within (id, day) used. lags One unique positive integer lag orders (mlVAR's lags). estimator Character. Frequentist estimator: "lmer" (multilevel) "lm" (separate person-specific models, requiring temporal = "unique"). legacy value "Mplus" selects engine = "mplus". temporal, contemporaneous Character random-effect structure. native frequentist engine supports fixed, correlated, orthogonal, unique person-specific effects. Bayesian engine maps correlated/orthogonal/ unique temporal effects full random-slope model. AR Logical. TRUE, estimate autoregressive (-lag) temporal effects, giving diagonal temporal matrix (matches mlVAR(AR = TRUE)). native frequentist/reference path requires estimator = "lmer". Default FALSE. scale Logical. TRUE, variable grand-mean centred divided pooled SD augmentation (mlVAR's scale). Default FALSE. (deprecated standardize alias.) scaleWithin Logical. TRUE, additionally scale within person (mlVAR's scaleWithin). Default FALSE. nCores Positive integer number outcome models fit parallel. Uses forked workers Unix-like systems PSOCK cluster Windows. verbose Logical. Emit progress messages. Default FALSE. lag Deprecated alias lags. standardize Deprecated alias scale. min_obs Integer NULL. Keep subjects least many observations (counts taken data). subject Optional vector naming exact subject(s) analyse. engine Estimation engine: "frequentist" (native lme4/base R), "bayes" (native DSEM sampler), "mplus" (licensed Mplus fit_mlvar_mplus()), "reference" (direct mlVAR::mlVAR() followed conversion idiographic's tidy result contract). standardize_mode Easy standardization vocabulary: "none", "global", "within", "". supplied sets scale scaleWithin; logical legacy arguments remain supported. missing Missing-data policy: "omit", "fail", "model". "fail" checks model variables ID/day/beep ordering keys. Within-model imputation available Bayesian random-slope engine. compare_to_lags Optional positive lag vector used align analysis rows comparing models different lag orders. must include every fitted value lags; example, use c(1, 2) fitting lag 1 comparison lag-2 model. true_means Optional data frame containing id vars, used known person means instead sample means. detrend "none" "position". Position detrending standardizes measurement position across subjects model standardization. na_rm Logical legacy spelling whether incomplete model rows omitted. FALSE equivalent missing = "fail". orthogonal Deprecated upstream compatibility flag. supplied sets temporal = "orthogonal" (TRUE) "correlated" (FALSE). ... Engine-specific controls. example n_iter, n_chains, residual Bayesian engine, Mplus controls Mplus engine.

title: Build a Multilevel Vector Autoregression (mlVAR) network — fit_mlvar
text: dual-class c("net_mlvar", "netobject_group") object — named list three full netobjects, one per network, plus model-level metadata stored attributes. element standard c("netobject", "cograph_network") weight-matrix wrapper (raw $data), print(), summary(), coefs(), cograph::splot(fit$temporal) work directly. three constituents matrix-wrapped carry underlying panel data, data-resampling workflow (bootstrap, reliability, stability) must start original panel rather wrappers. Structure: fit$temporal Directed netobject d x d matrix fixed-effect lagged coefficients. $weights[, j] effect variable j t-lag variable t. method = "mlvar_temporal", directed = TRUE. fit$contemporaneous Undirected netobject d x d partial-correlation network within-person lmer residuals. method = "mlvar_contemporaneous", directed = FALSE. fit$Undirected netobject d x d partial-correlation network person means, derived D (- Gamma). method = "mlvar_between", directed = FALSE. Convention: random-intercept SD 0 network estimable; idiographic returns -zero matrix (warning) plotting-oriented convention, whereas mlVAR returns -NA matrix. contemporaneous network follows zero--degeneracy convention. deliberate departure strict reference equivalence singular case. attr(fit, "coefs") / coefs() Tidy data.frame one row per (outcome, predictor) pair columns outcome, predictor, beta, se, t, p, ci_lower, ci_upper, significant. Filter, sort, plot base R tidyverse. Retrieve coefs(fit). attr(fit, "n_obs") Number rows augmented panel na.omit. attr(fit, "n_subjects") Number unique subjects remaining. attr(fit, "lag") Lag order used. attr(fit, "standardize") Logical; whether pre-augmentation standardization applied.

title: Build a Multilevel Vector Autoregression (mlVAR) network — fit_mlvar
text: algorithm follows mlVAR's lmer pipeline exactly: Drop rows NA id/day/beep optionally grand-mean standardize variable. Expand per-(id, day) beep grid right-join original values, producing augmented panel (augData). Add within-person lagged predictors (L1_*) person-mean predictors (PM_*). outcome variable fit lmer(y ~ within + -except--PM + (1 | id)) REML = FALSE. Collect fixed-effect temporal matrix B, -effect matrix Gamma, random-intercept SDs (mu_SD), lmer residual SDs. Contemporaneous network: cor2pcor(D %*% cov2cor(cor(resid)) %*% D). -subjects network: cor2pcor(pseudoinverse(forcePositive(D (- Gamma)))). committed oracle matrix validates fixed temporal/contemporaneous lmer fits lags 1 1+2, preprocessing controls (scale, scaleWithin, compareToLags, trueMeans, position detrending), lag-1 estimator = "lm", temporal = "unique" fits across every supported contemporaneous structure. configurations carry narrower declaration available equivalence().

title: Build a Multilevel Vector Autoregression (mlVAR) network — fit_mlvar
text: beep supplied, every complete (id, day, beep) key ((id, beep) day = NULL) must unique. Duplicate keys often indicate study-period/session column lost data conversion. upstream join behaviour row-order dependent case, fit_mlvar() errors asks resolve explicitly deduplicate source data.

title: Build a Multilevel Vector Autoregression (mlVAR) network — fit_mlvar
code: # \donttest{ set.seed(1) n_id <- 8; n_t <- 30; vars <- c("A", "B", "C") rows <- lapply(seq_len(n_id), function(i) {   m <- as.data.frame(matrix(rnorm(n_t * 3), ncol = 3))   names(m) <- vars   m$id <- i; m$day <- 1L; m$beep <- seq_len(n_t)   m }) d <- do.call(rbind, rows) fit <- fit_mlvar(d, vars = vars, id = "id", day = "day", beep = "beep") #> Warning: Model for 'A': singular fit (random-effects variance near zero). #> Warning: Model for 'A': boundary (singular) fit: see help('isSingular') #> Warning: Model for 'B': singular fit (random-effects variance near zero). #> Warning: Model for 'B': boundary (singular) fit: see help('isSingular') #> Warning: Model for 'C': singular fit (random-effects variance near zero). #> Warning: Model for 'C': boundary (singular) fit: see help('isSingular') #> Warning: Between-subjects network not estimable: a random-intercept SD is 0 (no between-person variance). Returning a zero matrix by convention (mlVAR returns NA here). print(fit) #> mlVAR result: 8 subjects, 232 observations, 3 variables (lags 1) #>   Temporal edges significant at p<0.05: 1 / 9 #>  #>   Temporal [directed] #>     weights [-0.131, 0.062]  |  +3 / -6 edges #>           A     B     C #>     A -0.13 -0.05 -0.03 #>     B -0.01 -0.08 -0.01 #>     C  0.06  0.02  0.04 #>  #>   Contemporaneous [undirected] #>     weights [0.006, 0.085]  |  +3 / -0 edges #>          A    B    C #>     A 0.00 0.06 0.08 #>     B 0.06 0.00 0.01 #>     C 0.08 0.01 0.00 #>  #>   Between [undirected] #>     no non-zero edges #>       A B C #>     A 0 0 0 #>     B 0 0 0 #>     C 0 0 0 #>  #>   plot(x) | plot(x, layer = "temporal") | plot(x, layer = "between")  #>   edges(x) | nodes(x) | summary(x) | coefs(x) | matrices(x) summary(fit) #>           network n_nodes n_edges density mean_abs_weight n_positive n_negative #> 1        temporal       3       6       1      0.03140474          2          4 #> 2 contemporaneous       3       3       1      0.05004674          3          0 #> 3         between       3       0       0      0.00000000          0          0 # }

what: Build a Bayesian multilevel VAR network (Mplus DSEM-targeted) — fit_mlvar_bayes
title: Build a Bayesian multilevel VAR network (Mplus DSEM-targeted) — fit_mlvar_bayes
text: Native, pure-R Bayesian estimator two-level VAR(1) statistically reproduces Mplus DSEM output (estimator behind mlVAR::mlVAR(estimator = "Mplus")) without needing Mplus installed. conjugate Gibbs sampler estimates fixed temporal matrix, within-person residual (contemporaneous) network, -person network, using latent mean centring Mplus's default priors. Point estimates posterior medians posterior SDs 95% credible intervals.

title: Build a Bayesian multilevel VAR network (Mplus DSEM-targeted) — fit_mlvar_bayes
code: fit_mlvar_bayes(   data,   vars,   id,   day = NULL,   beep = NULL,   lags = 1L,   temporal = c("fixed", "default", "random"),   contemporaneous = c("fixed", "default"),   residual = c("fixed", "random"),   scale = TRUE,   scaleWithin = FALSE,   tinterval = NULL,   impute = FALSE,   n_iter = 4000L,   n_burnin = NULL,   n_chains = 2L,   thin = 1L,   seed = NULL,   min_obs = NULL,   subject = NULL,   verbose = FALSE )

title: Build a Bayesian multilevel VAR network (Mplus DSEM-targeted) — fit_mlvar_bayes
text: data data.frame containing panel data. vars Character vector variable column names model (length >= 2). id Character string naming person-ID column. day Character string naming day/session column, NULL. beep Character string naming measurement-occasion column, NULL. NULL, row position within (id, day) block used. lags Integer lag order; 1 supported (matches Mplus DSEM defaults ). temporal Character. "fixed" (default) fits fixed temporal effects random intercepts (Mplus DSEM temporal = "fixed"). "random" fits full DSEM person-specific temporal matrices B_i full random-effect covariance (mu_i, vec(B_i)); temporal network reports posterior mean transition matrix attr(fit, "slope_sd") holds per-coefficient random-slope SDs. "random" needs subjects estimable random-effect covariance: least 2 * (p + p^2) + 1 subjects. contemporaneous Character. "fixed" implemented. residual Character. "fixed" (default) uses one shared population within-person residual covariance. "random" (temporal = "random") gives subject residual covariance Sigma_W_i via conjugate hierarchical inverse-Wishart (Sigma_W_i ~ IW(Lambda, p + 2), Lambda ~ Wishart), matching DSEM person-specific innovation variances; reported contemporaneous network population-average residual covariance. scale Logical. Global grand-mean/SD standardization variable fitting (Mplus/mlVAR scale = TRUE). Default TRUE. scaleWithin Logical. Additionally within-person scale variable. Default FALSE. tinterval Numeric NULL. supplied, beep treated continuous time variable binned onto regular grid width (Mplus TINTERVAL); integer bin becomes occasion index gap-aware lagging, multiple observations one (id, day, bin) slot collapsed first. Lagging gap-aware cases: lag-1 pairs formed consecutive occasions, missing occasions never create spurious lag pairs. Default NULL. impute Logical. TRUE (temporal = "random"), missing observations imputed within model MCMC iteration (data augmentation), rather dropped: person's series expanded full occasion grid every latent cell drawn Gaussian full conditional (outcome t predictor t+1), using vectorised even/odd (checkerboard) block sweep. matches Mplus / Stan / JAGS handle missing data removes listwise-deletion bias heavy missingness, extra computational cost. Default FALSE. n_iter Integer. Total MCMC iterations per chain. Default 4000. n_burnin Integer. Burn-iterations discarded per chain. Default n_iter / 2 (Mplus's first-half burn-convention). n_chains Integer. Number independent chains. Default 2. thin Integer. Keep every thin-th post-burn-draw. Default 1. seed Integer NULL. Base RNG seed (chain c uses seed + c). min_obs Integer NULL. Keep subjects least many observations fitting. subject Optional vector naming exact subject(s) analyse. verbose Logical. Emit progress messages. Default FALSE.

title: Build a Bayesian multilevel VAR network (Mplus DSEM-targeted) — fit_mlvar_bayes
text: net_mlvar_bayes object (also inheriting net_mlvar), named list three netobjects (temporal, contemporaneous, ) posterior-summary attributes. coefs() returns tidy table estimate (posterior median), posterior_sd, ci_lower, ci_upper, p (one-tailed), significant (95% CI excludes 0). Posterior draws max Gelman-Rubin PSR kept attributes.

title: Build a Bayesian multilevel VAR network (Mplus DSEM-targeted) — fit_mlvar_bayes
text: sampler alternates five conjugate full-conditional draws per iteration: latent person means mu_i (Gaussian), fixed temporal matrix B (matrix-normal), within residual covariance Sigma_W (inverse-Wishart), grand mean alpha (Gaussian), covariance Sigma_B (inverse-Wishart). lagged predictor recentred current mu_i draw every iteration (latent mean centring). Data globally standardized first (matching mlVAR's scale = TRUE); first observation block used lag (condition--first). Validated statistical (Monte-Carlo-error) equivalence real Mplus 9 DSEM output standardized synthetic panels: posterior medians B, Sigma_W, Sigma_B agree Mplus well within posterior SD.

title: Build a Bayesian multilevel VAR network (Mplus DSEM-targeted) — fit_mlvar_bayes
code: # \donttest{ set.seed(1) n_id <- 10; n_t <- 40; vars <- c("A", "B") rows <- lapply(seq_len(n_id), function(i) {   y <- matrix(0, n_t, 2)   for (t in 2:n_t) y[t, ] <- c(0.3, 0.15) * y[t - 1, ] + rnorm(2)   data.frame(id = i, beep = seq_len(n_t), A = y[, 1], B = y[, 2]) }) d <- do.call(rbind, rows) fit <- fit_mlvar_bayes(d, vars = vars, id = "id", beep = "beep",                          n_iter = 2000, seed = 1) print(fit) #> Bayesian mlVAR (Mplus DSEM-targeted, temporal = fixed): 10 subjects, 390 observations, 2 variables #>   MCMC: 2 chains x 2000 iter (1000 burn-in), 2000 draws | max PSR = 1.003 #>   Temporal 95% CIs excluding 0: 2 / 4 #>  #>   Temporal [directed] #>     weights [-0.083, 0.256]  |  +2 / -2 edges #>           A     B #>     A  0.26 -0.08 #>     B -0.07  0.13 #>  #>   Contemporaneous [undirected] #>     weights [-0.005, -0.005]  |  +0 / -1 edges #>       A B #>     A 0 0 #>     B 0 0 #>  #>   Between [undirected] #>     weights [0.201, 0.201]  |  +1 / -0 edges #>         A   B #>     A 0.0 0.2 #>     B 0.2 0.0 #>  #>   coefs(x) posterior median/SD/CI | matrices(x) | edges(x) | summary(x) coefs(fit) #>   outcome predictor    estimate posterior_sd    ci_lower   ci_upper     p #> 1       A         A  0.25617841   0.05061229  0.15903046 0.35618498 0.000 #> 2       A         B -0.07096325   0.05116161 -0.17225969 0.03004842 0.078 #> 3       B         A -0.08325036   0.05187507 -0.18856429 0.01414300 0.045 #> 4       B         B  0.12607025   0.05115064  0.02490567 0.22468417 0.009 #>   significant #> 1        TRUE #> 2       FALSE #> 3       FALSE #> 4        TRUE # }

what: Build an Mplus-backed multilevel VAR network — fit_mlvar_mplus
title: Build an Mplus-backed multilevel VAR network — fit_mlvar_mplus
text: Runs Mplus Bayesian estimator exposed mlVAR::mlVAR(estimator = "Mplus") converts returned posterior summaries idiographic's network/tidy accessors. true Mplus backend: Mplus must installed discoverable MplusAutomation::detectMplus().

title: Build an Mplus-backed multilevel VAR network — fit_mlvar_mplus
code: fit_mlvar_mplus(   data,   vars,   id,   day = NULL,   beep = NULL,   lags = 1L,   temporal = c("fixed", "correlated", "orthogonal", "default"),   contemporaneous = c("fixed", "correlated", "orthogonal", "default"),   nCores = 1L,   scale = TRUE,   scaleWithin = FALSE,   MplusSave = TRUE,   MplusName = "mlVAR_mplus",   iterations = "(2000)",   chains = nCores,   signs,   min_obs = NULL,   subject = NULL,   workdir = NULL,   verbose = TRUE,   ... )

title: Build an Mplus-backed multilevel VAR network — fit_mlvar_mplus
text: data data.frame containing panel data. vars Character vector variable column names model. id Character string naming person-ID column. day Character string naming day/session column, NULL. Mplus estimation mlVAR directly support day; supplied passed mlVAR can prepare row order, mlVAR warn Mplus limitation. beep Character string naming measurement-occasion column, NULL. lags Integer lag order. Mplus backend currently supports 1. temporal, contemporaneous Random-effect structure passed mlVAR. Supported Mplus values "fixed", "correlated", "orthogonal", "default". nCores Number Mplus processors/chains. scale, scaleWithin Standardization options passed mlVAR. MplusSave Logical. Keep Mplus input/output files working directory? Default TRUE. MplusName File stem Mplus input/output files. iterations Mplus BITERATIONS string, e.g. "(2000)". chains Number Mplus chains. Defaults nCores. signs Optional sign matrix contemporaneous random effects. min_obs Integer NULL. Keep subjects least many observations fitting. subject Optional vector naming exact subject(s) analyse. workdir Directory Mplus files written/run. Default uses current working directory. verbose Logical. Show progress mlVAR/Mplus. ... Additional arguments passed mlVAR::mlVAR().

title: Build an Mplus-backed multilevel VAR network — fit_mlvar_mplus
text: net_mplus object, also inheriting net_mlvar, temporal, contemporaneous, networks plus Mplus metadata attributes. original mlVAR/Mplus object available attr(x, "mplus").

title: Build an Mplus-backed multilevel VAR network — fit_mlvar_mplus
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ fit <- fit_mlvar_mplus(   data, vars = c("A", "B", "C"), id = "id", beep = "time",   temporal = "fixed", contemporaneous = "fixed",   MplusName = "my_mplus_mlvar" ) edges(fit) attr(fit, "mplus")$output$summaries } # }

what: Estimate rolling-window graphical VAR networks — fit_rolling_graphical_var
title: Estimate rolling-window graphical VAR networks — fit_rolling_graphical_var
text: Fits fit_graphical_var() ordered, overlapping windows within subject. time-varying graphical VAR companion fit_rolling_var(): every window uses graphical VAR's lag construction, EBIC/penalty settings, tidy coefficient access, returns one coefficient table per window.

title: Estimate rolling-window graphical VAR networks — fit_rolling_graphical_var
code: fit_rolling_graphical_var(   data,   vars,   id = NULL,   day = NULL,   beep = NULL,   window_size,   step = 1L,   scale = TRUE,   center_within = TRUE,   delete_missings = TRUE,   min_obs = NULL,   subject = NULL,   keep_fits = FALSE,   ... )

title: Estimate rolling-window graphical VAR networks — fit_rolling_graphical_var
text: data data.frame matrix columns variables optional id/day/beep columns. vars Character vector variable names. id Character. Name person-ID column, NULL single series. day Character. Name day/session column, NULL. beep Character. Name measurement-occasion column, NULL. window_size Integer number ordered rows per rolling window. step Integer number rows advance windows. Default 1. scale Logical. Whether standardize variables inside window. Default TRUE. center_within Logical. Whether centre within person inside window one id present. Default TRUE. delete_missings Logical. Drop incomplete current/lagged rows. Default TRUE. min_obs Integer NULL. Keep subjects least many observations rolling. subject Optional vector naming subject(s) analyse. keep_fits Logical. Store successful gvar_result fits? Default FALSE. ... arguments passed fit_graphical_var(), n_lambda, gamma, lambda_beta, lambda_kappa.

title: Estimate rolling-window graphical VAR networks — fit_rolling_graphical_var
text: rolling_gvar_result $estimates, $windows, $failures, optionally $fits. $estimates tidy coefficient table subject/window metadata plus network, , , weight.

title: Estimate rolling-window graphical VAR networks — fit_rolling_graphical_var
code: set.seed(1) d <- data.frame(id = 1, day = rep(1:5, each = 20),                 beep = rep(1:20, 5),                 A = rnorm(100), B = rnorm(100), C = rnorm(100)) tv <- fit_rolling_graphical_var(d, vars = c("A", "B", "C"), id = "id",                             day = "day", beep = "beep",                             window_size = 50, step = 25,                             scale = FALSE, n_lambda = 5) head(tv$estimates) #>   subject window start_row end_row start_day end_day start_beep end_beep #> 1       1      1         1      50         1       3          1       10 #> 2       1      1         1      50         1       3          1       10 #> 3       1      1         1      50         1       3          1       10 #> 4       1      1         1      50         1       3          1       10 #> 5       1      1         1      50         1       3          1       10 #> 6       1      1         1      50         1       3          1       10 #>    network from to weight #> 1 temporal    A  A      0 #> 2 temporal    B  A      0 #> 3 temporal    C  A      0 #> 4 temporal    A  B      0 #> 5 temporal    B  B      0 #> 6 temporal    C  B      0

what: Build a user-specified unified SEM network — fit_usem
title: Build a user-specified unified SEM network — fit_usem
text: Fits person-specific unified Structural Equation Models (uSEM) intensive longitudinal data. uSEM combines lagged directed effects, optional contemporaneous directed effects, optional residual covariances one SEM. Unlike fit_gimme(), function automated path search: model fixed temporal, contemporaneous, residual_cov, paths. trim = TRUE, idiographic uses independent clean-room modification-index entry z-value pruning layer declared candidate set.

title: Build a user-specified unified SEM network — fit_usem
code: fit_usem(   data,   vars,   id,   time = NULL,   day = NULL,   beep = NULL,   min_obs = NULL,   subject = NULL,   temporal = c("ar", "all", "none"),   contemporaneous = c("none", "all"),   residual_cov = TRUE,   trim = FALSE,   trim_alpha = 0.05,   trim_fit_criteria = 3L,   cfi_cutoff = 0.95,   tli_cutoff = 0.95,   rmsea_cutoff = 0.08,   srmr_cutoff = 0.08,   paths = NULL,   exogenous = NULL,   standardize = FALSE,   estimator = "ml",   seed = NULL )

title: Build a user-specified unified SEM network — fit_usem
text: data data.frame long format. vars Character vector time-varying variables. id Character string naming person-ID column. time Character string naming within-person ordering column, NULL. day Character string naming day/session column, NULL. supplied, lag pairs formed within (id, day) block. beep Character string naming measurement-occasion column, NULL. Used day order observations time supplied. min_obs Integer NULL. Keep subjects least many observations. subject Optional vector naming subject(s) analyse. temporal "ar" (-lag ; default), "" (lagged predictors), "none", character vector lavaan regressions "~ Blag". contemporaneous "none" (default), "" (directed lag-0 predictors except self-regressions), lavaan regressions "B ~ ". residual_cov Logical. Estimate residual covariances among current endogenous variables? Default TRUE. trim Logical. TRUE, treat temporal, contemporaneous, residual_cov eligible search space: start structural baseline, add paths modification index fit criteria met, prune weak paths. idiographic clean-room search layer, clone external package. Default FALSE fits exact fixed syntax. trim_alpha Significance level used modification-index entry z-value pruning trim = TRUE. Default 0.05. trim_fit_criteria Number fit criteria must pass forward search stops. Default 3. cfi_cutoff, tli_cutoff, rmsea_cutoff, srmr_cutoff Fit thresholds used trimmed uSEM. paths Extra lavaan syntax lines include unchanged. exogenous Optional subset vars treat exogenous current variables. can predict endogenous variables outcomes. standardize Logical. Standardize variables per person fitting. estimator Lavaan estimator. Default "ml". seed Optional random seed.

title: Build a user-specified unified SEM network — fit_usem
text: net_usem object average $temporal, $contemporaneous, $residual_cov matrices, per-subject matrices $subjects, tidy coefficient table coefs(), fit indices, syntax, labels, configuration metadata.

title: Build a user-specified unified SEM network — fit_usem
code: # \donttest{ set.seed(1) d <- data.frame(   id = rep(1:4, each = 30),   t = rep(seq_len(30), 4),   A = rnorm(120), B = rnorm(120), C = rnorm(120) ) fit <- fit_usem(d, vars = c("A", "B", "C"), id = "id", time = "t") edges(fit) #>        network from to      weight #> 1 residual_cov    B  C -0.12338761 #> 2 residual_cov    A  C  0.09763793 #> 3 residual_cov    A  B  0.04235390 # }

what: Build an ordinary least-squares VAR network — fit_var
title: Build an ordinary least-squares VAR network — fit_var
text: Fits transparent VAR(1) baseline intensive longitudinal data using ordinary least squares: current variables regressed intercept lag-1 predictors. lag construction, scaling, within-person centring, day-boundary behaviour match fit_graphical_var(), regularization EBIC model selection applied.

title: Build an ordinary least-squares VAR network — fit_var
code: fit_var(   data,   vars,   id = NULL,   day = NULL,   beep = NULL,   lags = 1L,   scale = TRUE,   center_within = TRUE,   delete_missings = TRUE,   min_obs = NULL,   subject = NULL )

title: Build an ordinary least-squares VAR network — fit_var
text: data data.frame matrix columns variables optional id/day/beep columns. vars Character vector variable names. id Character. Name person-ID column, NULL single series. day Character. Name day/session column, NULL. beep Character. Name measurement-occasion column, NULL. lags Integer. 1 supported. scale Logical. Whether standardize variables lagging. Default TRUE. center_within Logical. Whether centre within person one id present. Default TRUE. delete_missings Logical. Drop incomplete current/lagged rows. Default TRUE. min_obs Integer NULL. Keep subjects least many observations. subject Optional vector naming subject(s) analyse.

title: Build an ordinary least-squares VAR network — fit_var
text: var_result object temporal OLS coefficients, residual covariance, residual precision, contemporaneous partial correlations, tidy access edges(), coefs(), nodes(), summary().

title: Build an ordinary least-squares VAR network — fit_var
code: set.seed(1) d <- data.frame(id = 1, A = rnorm(80), B = rnorm(80), C = rnorm(80)) fit <- fit_var(d, vars = c("A", "B", "C"), id = "id") edges(fit) #>           network from to       weight #> 1        temporal    C  B  0.113592795 #> 2        temporal    B  C -0.056084686 #> 3        temporal    B  A  0.042216979 #> 4        temporal    A  B -0.030796224 #> 5        temporal    A  C -0.024626586 #> 6        temporal    C  A  0.004625333 #> 7 contemporaneous    A  B -0.302794840 #> 8 contemporaneous    A  C  0.175739891 #> 9 contemporaneous    B  C  0.042015104

what: Build a Bayesian VAR(1) network (unregularized, Mplus-targeted) — fit_var_bayes
title: Build a Bayesian VAR(1) network (unregularized, Mplus-targeted) — fit_var_bayes
text: Native, pure-R Bayesian VAR(1) reproduces Mplus's Bayesian (DSEM/time-series) estimates without needing Mplus. unregularized Bayesian counterpart fit_graphical_var(): instead graphical-lasso / EBIC sparse fit, estimates full VAR(1) flat prior temporal coefficients inverse-Wishart prior residual precision, reports temporal network B contemporaneous partial-correlation network derived residual covariance. one subject data within-person centred pooled (fit_graphical_var()).

title: Build a Bayesian VAR(1) network (unregularized, Mplus-targeted) — fit_var_bayes
code: fit_var_bayes(   data,   vars,   id = NULL,   day = NULL,   beep = NULL,   lags = 1L,   scale = TRUE,   center_within = TRUE,   n_iter = 4000L,   n_burnin = NULL,   n_chains = 2L,   thin = 1L,   seed = NULL,   min_obs = NULL,   subject = NULL,   verbose = FALSE )

title: Build a Bayesian VAR(1) network (unregularized, Mplus-targeted) — fit_var_bayes
text: data data.frame matrix. vars Character vector variable names (length >= 2). id Character. Person-ID column, NULL single series. day Character. Day/session column, NULL. beep Character. Beep/measurement column, NULL. lags Integer lag order; 1 supported. scale Logical. Global standardization variable. Default TRUE. center_within Logical. Within-person centre >1 id (removes -person variance, fit_graphical_var()). Default TRUE. n_iter, n_burnin, n_chains, thin MCMC controls. Defaults 4000, n_iter/2, 2, 1. seed Integer NULL. Base seed (chain c uses seed + c). min_obs Integer NULL. Keep subjects least many observations. subject Optional vector naming exact subject(s) analyse. verbose Logical. Progress messages. Default FALSE.

title: Build a Bayesian VAR(1) network (unregularized, Mplus-targeted) — fit_var_bayes
text: var_bayes_result object (cograph group temporal contemporaneous netobjects) carrying beta, temporal, kappa, PCC, PDC, posterior draws, tidy coefs() table (posterior median, SD, 95% CI, one-tailed p, significance CI excluding 0).

title: Build a Bayesian VAR(1) network (unregularized, Mplus-targeted) — fit_var_bayes
code: # \donttest{ set.seed(1) y <- matrix(0, 200, 2) for (t in 2:200) y[t, ] <- c(0.4, 0.3) * y[t - 1, ] + rnorm(2) d <- data.frame(A = y[, 1], B = y[, 2]) fit <- fit_var_bayes(d, vars = c("A", "B"), n_iter = 2000, seed = 1) print(fit) #> Bayesian VAR(1) result (unregularized, Mplus-targeted) #>   Variables:    2 (A, B) #>   Observations: 199 #>   MCMC: 2 chains x 2000 iter, 2000 draws | max PSR = 1.000 #>   Temporal 95% CIs excluding 0: 2 / 4 #>  #>   Temporal [directed] #>     weights [-0.062, 0.427]  |  +2 / -2 edges #>           A     B #>     A  0.43 -0.05 #>     B -0.06  0.27 #>  #>   Contemporaneous [undirected] #>     weights [-0.015, -0.015]  |  +0 / -1 edges #>           A     B #>     A  0.00 -0.01 #>     B -0.01  0.00 #>  #>   coefs(x) | matrices(x) | edges(x) | nodes(x) | summary(x) coefs(fit) #>   outcome predictor    estimate posterior_sd   ci_lower   ci_upper     p #> 1       A         A  0.42710673   0.06610752  0.2910596 0.55296236 0.000 #> 2       A         B -0.06167479   0.06497069 -0.1926188 0.06364944 0.178 #> 3       B         A -0.05095663   0.07205346 -0.1961841 0.08994568 0.242 #> 4       B         B  0.27067298   0.07000427  0.1327740 0.40495916 0.000 #>   significant #> 1        TRUE #> 2       FALSE #> 3       FALSE #> 4        TRUE # }

Name day/session column, NULL. beep Character. Name measurement-occasion column, NULL. min_obs Integer NULL. Keep subjects least many observations. ... arguments passed fit_var().

title: Fit an ordinary least-squares VAR for every subject — fit_var_each

title: Fit an ordinary least-squares VAR for every subject — fit_var_each
code: set.seed(1) d <- data.frame(   id = rep(1:3, each = 40),   day = rep(1, 120),   beep = rep(seq_len(40), 3),   A = rnorm(120), B = rnorm(120), C = rnorm(120) ) fits <- fit_var_each(d, vars = c("A", "B", "C"), id = "id",                        day = "day", beep = "beep") fits[["1"]] #> OLS VAR Result #>   Variables:      3 (A, B, C) #>   Observations:   39 #>   Temporal edges: 9 / 9 #>   Contemp edges:  3 / 3 #>  #>   Temporal [directed] #>     weights [-0.334, 0.123]  |  +3 / -6 edges #>           A     B     C #>     A  0.07  0.12  0.08 #>     B -0.33 -0.31 -0.10 #>     C -0.27 -0.07 -0.29 #>  #>   Contemporaneous [undirected] #>     weights [-0.236, 0.043]  |  +1 / -2 edges #>           A     B     C #>     A  0.00  0.04 -0.01 #>     B  0.04  0.00 -0.24 #>     C -0.01 -0.24  0.00 #>  #>   plot(x) | plot(x, layer = "temporal")  #>   edges(x) | nodes(x) | summary(x) | coefs(x) | matrices(x)
