what: idiographic: Idiographic Network Estimation from Intensive Longitudinal Data — idiographic-package
title: idiographic: Idiographic Network Estimation from Intensive Longitudinal Data — idiographic-package
text: Person-specific within-person network estimation intensive longitudinal / ESM panel data: preprocessing audits (preprocess()), edge-stability diagnostics (estimate_stability()), model-comparison reports (compare_idiographic()), rolling forecast validation (validate_forecast()), rolling ordinary vector autoregression (fit_rolling_var()), rolling graphical vector autoregression (fit_rolling_graphical_var()), ordinary vector autoregression (fit_var()), graphical vector autoregression (fit_graphical_var()), multilevel vector autoregression (fit_mlvar()), unified SEM (fit_usem()), Group Iterative Multiple Model Estimation (fit_gimme()), idiographic supervised machine-learning models (fit_ml()) individualized prediction. Use fit_idiographic() registry-driven dispatch direct fit_*() functions. Every result tidy .data.frame(), summary(), print methods; network estimators also share edges(), coefs(), nodes(), matrices(), plotting methods. equivalence() reports exact validation scope attached method.

title: idiographic: Idiographic Network Estimation from Intensive Longitudinal Data — idiographic-package
text: Maintainer: Mohammed Saqr saqr@saqr.[copyright holder] Authors: Sonsoles López-Pernas sonsoles.lopez@uef.fi

code: W <- matrix(c(0, 0.3, -0.2, 0), 2, 2,             dimnames = list(c("A", "B"), c("A", "B"))) x <- structure(list(weights = W, method = "relative", directed = TRUE),                class = "cograph_network") nodes(as_netobject(x)) #>    network node strength out_strength in_strength self #> A relative    A      0.5          0.2         0.3    0 #> B relative    B      0.5          0.3         0.2    0

what: Faithful GIMME network plot (the gimme-package convention, via cograph) — plot_gimme
title: Faithful GIMME network plot (the gimme-package convention, via cograph) — plot_gimme
text: Draws GIMME result way gimme package : single p-node network dashed edges lag-1 (temporal) solid edges lag-0 (contemporaneous), edge width proportion subjects path, black edges group-level paths grey edges individual-level, autoregression shows dashed self-loop. Rendered cograph::splot(), lag contemporaneous effect pair drawn two parallel edges.

Defaults "black" / "grey60". layout cograph layout passed cograph::splot(). Default "circle", matching gimme. curvature Edge curvature (separates parallel lag/contemp edges). Default 0.25. edge_scale Multiplier mapping weight drawn line width. Default 5. ... arguments forwarded cograph::splot().

title: Plot an idiographic network result — plot_idiographic
text: x idiographic result (var_result, gvar_result, net_mlvar, net_usem, net_gimme, var_list, rolling_var_result, rolling_gvar_result, stability_result). layer Optional network name draw . NULL (default) draws whole result. Available names reported unknown one given. mixed TRUE, draw two layers single mixed network via cograph::plot_mixed_network() — directed layer curved arrows undirected layer straight edges. VAR, graphical VAR, multilevel VAR combines directed temporal network undirected contemporaneous network; uSEM combines directed contemporaneous paths undirected residual covariances. Default FALSE draws one panel per layer. ... arguments forwarded cograph::splot(). weight GIMME: "prop" (proportion subjects, default) "coef" (group-average coefficient) edge width. subject var_list / gvar_list: subject name (index) draw. Defaults first subject. fit rolling results: stored window fit (name index) draw. Requires keep_fits = TRUE fit time. Defaults first window.

what: Preprocess and audit idiographic time-series data — preprocess
title: Preprocess and audit idiographic time-series data — preprocess
text: Builds lag-1 design used fit_graphical_var() fit_var(), optionally detrends differences series, returns tidy diagnostics missingness, day-boundary drops, simple linear trends, AR(1) persistence, split-half mean/variance drift, ADF-style unit-root screen, zero-variance variables. makes modelling input explicit estimating VAR, graphical VAR, uSEM, GIMME, mlVAR models; detrend also cleans non-stationary series place flags can rechecked transformed data.

title: Preprocess and audit idiographic time-series data — preprocess
code: preprocess(   data,   vars,   id = NULL,   day = NULL,   beep = NULL,   scale = TRUE,   center_within = TRUE,   detrend = "none",   checks = c("trend", "high_ar", "unit_root", "mean_shift", "sd_shift", "zero_variance"),   delete_missings = TRUE,   min_obs = NULL,   subject = NULL,   trend_alpha = 0.05,   ar_threshold = 0.95,   mean_shift_threshold = 0.8,   sd_ratio_threshold = 2,   unit_root_t_cutoff = -2.86 )

title: Preprocess and audit idiographic time-series data — preprocess
text: data data.frame matrix columns variables optional id/day/beep columns. vars Character vector variable names. id Character. Name person-ID column, NULL single series. day Character. Name day/session column, NULL. beep Character. Name measurement-occasion column, NULL. scale Logical. Whether standardize variables lagging. Default TRUE. center_within Logical. Whether centre within person one id present. Default TRUE. detrend remove non-stationarity series lagging. Either single string applied every variable, named character vector giving per-variable method (unlisted variables left untouched, e.g. c(planning = "difference", value = "linear")). available methods : "none" Default; diagnose , transform nothing. "auto" Detrend subject-series flagged, leaving stationary ones untouched: differencing stochastic trend (unit root near-unit-root persistence) linearly detrending deterministic trend. "clean whoever needs " option – subsetting, one call everyone. Can set per variable . "linear" Replace series residuals within-person regression linear time index. "difference" First-difference series within id/day blocks. diagnostics returned design reflect detrended series, trend unit-root flags can rechecked cleaning. checks Character vector selecting stationarity checkups run: "trend", "high_ar", "unit_root", "mean_shift", "sd_shift", "zero_variance". Defaults . Deselecting check turns flag report, flag_stationarity_risk roll-, "auto" detrend decision, can screen care . delete_missings Logical. TRUE, $pairs contains complete current/lagged rows; FALSE, first rows blocks incomplete rows retained NA lags, matching .gvar_tsdata(). Default TRUE. min_obs Integer NULL. Keep subjects least many observations. subject Optional vector naming subject(s) preprocess. trend_alpha Numeric p-value cutoff trend flag. Default 0.05. ar_threshold Numeric absolute AR(1) cutoff high-persistence flag. Default 0.95. mean_shift_threshold Numeric absolute standardized split-half mean shift cutoff. Default 0.8. sd_ratio_threshold Numeric split-half SD ratio cutoff. Default 2. unit_root_t_cutoff Numeric cutoff ADF-style lag-level t-statistic. Values greater cutoff flagged unit-root risk. Default -2.86, common large-sample intercept-screening cutoff.

title: Preprocess and audit idiographic time-series data — preprocess
text: preprocess_result object : pairs ordered current/lagged design table, including intercept L1_* columns. counts Per-subject/per-day row lag-pair counts. diagnostics Per-subject/per-variable missingness, trend, AR(1), split-half drift, unit-root screen, stationarity risk indicators. matrices exact data_c data_l matrices returned VAR/GVAR preprocessing path.

title: Preprocess and audit idiographic time-series data — preprocess
code: set.seed(1) d <- data.frame(id = 1, day = 1, beep = 1:40,                 A = cumsum(rnorm(40)), B = rnorm(40)) pp <- preprocess(d, vars = c("A", "B"), id = "id", day = "day", beep = "beep") #> 1 of 2 subject-series show a trend or unit-root that can bias the temporal network. preprocess() only diagnosed this; to clean just the series that need it, re-run with: #>   preprocess(data = d, vars = c("A", "B"), id = "id", day = "day", beep = "beep", detrend = "auto") pp$counts #>   subject day n_rows n_lag_possible n_complete_pairs n_retained #> 1       1   1     40             39               39         39 #>   n_boundary_dropped #> 1                  1 pp$diagnostics #>   subject variable  n n_observed missing_prop         mean sd  trend_slope #> 1       1        A 40         40            0 2.637593e-17  1  0.054326088 #> 2       1        B 40         40            0 1.507583e-17  1 -0.008082785 #>      trend_t      trend_p        ar1      ar1_t        ar1_p mean_first_half #> 1  5.0683995 1.072035e-05  0.8271452 10.1409016 3.128890e-12     -0.65545859 #> 2 -0.5851021 5.619376e-01 -0.1054387 -0.6407008 5.256656e-01      0.02001441 #>   mean_second_half  mean_shift mean_shift_std mean_shift_p sd_first_half #> 1       0.65545859  1.31091717     1.31091717 3.485167e-06     0.8382505 #> 2      -0.02001441 -0.04002882     0.04002882 9.012450e-01     0.8745922 #>   sd_second_half sd_ratio unit_root_coef unit_root_t flag_zero_variance #> 1      0.6674518 1.255897     -0.1728548   -2.119221              FALSE #> 2      1.1344059 1.297068     -1.1054387   -6.717223              FALSE #>   flag_trend flag_high_ar flag_mean_shift flag_sd_shift flag_unit_root #> 1       TRUE        FALSE            TRUE         FALSE           TRUE #> 2      FALSE        FALSE           FALSE         FALSE          FALSE #>   flag_stationarity_risk #> 1                   TRUE #> 2                  FALSE # Difference the trending series and recheck the flags: preprocess(d, vars = c("A", "B"), id = "id", day = "day", beep = "beep",            detrend = "difference")$diagnostics #>   subject variable  n n_observed missing_prop        mean        sd #> 1       1        A 40         39        0.025  0.06550595 0.5281272 #> 2       1        B 40         39        0.025 -0.01177038 1.4990982 #>    trend_slope    trend_t   trend_p         ar1      ar1_t        ar1_p #> 1 -0.004133590 -0.5450014 0.5890229  0.03985218  0.2375795 0.8135542319 #> 2 -0.004349215 -0.2013219 0.8415496 -0.54633732 -3.8973207 0.0004063707 #>   mean_first_half mean_second_half  mean_shift mean_shift_std mean_shift_p #> 1     0.138499769     -0.003838187 -0.14233796     0.26951451    0.4081080 #> 2     0.001675254     -0.024543733 -0.02621899     0.01748984    0.9570363 #>   sd_first_half sd_second_half sd_ratio unit_root_coef unit_root_t #> 1      0.544007      0.5167972 1.052651     -0.9601478   -5.723937 #> 2      1.293586      1.7055629 1.318476     -1.5463373  -11.030864 #>   flag_zero_variance flag_trend flag_high_ar flag_mean_shift flag_sd_shift #> 1              FALSE      FALSE        FALSE           FALSE         FALSE #> 2              FALSE      FALSE        FALSE           FALSE         FALSE #>   flag_unit_root flag_stationarity_risk #> 1          FALSE                  FALSE #> 2          FALSE                  FALSE

title: Print method for net_mlvar — print.net_mlvar
code: # \donttest{ set.seed(1) n_id <- 8; n_t <- 30; vars <- c("A", "B", "C") rows <- lapply(seq_len(n_id), function(i) {   m <- as.data.frame(matrix(rnorm(n_t * 3), ncol = 3))   names(m) <- vars   m$id <- i; m$day <- 1L; m$beep <- seq_len(n_t)   m }) d <- do.call(rbind, rows) fit <- fit_mlvar(d, vars = vars, id = "id", day = "day", beep = "beep") #> Warning: Model for 'A': singular fit (random-effects variance near zero). #> Warning: Model for 'A': boundary (singular) fit: see help('isSingular') #> Warning: Model for 'B': singular fit (random-effects variance near zero). #> Warning: Model for 'B': boundary (singular) fit: see help('isSingular') #> Warning: Model for 'C': singular fit (random-effects variance near zero). #> Warning: Model for 'C': boundary (singular) fit: see help('isSingular') #> Warning: Between-subjects network not estimable: a random-intercept SD is 0 (no between-person variance). Returning a zero matrix by convention (mlVAR returns NA here). print(fit) #> mlVAR result: 8 subjects, 232 observations, 3 variables (lags 1) #>   Temporal edges significant at p<0.05: 1 / 9 #>  #>   Temporal [directed] #>     weights [-0.131, 0.062]  |  +3 / -6 edges #>           A     B     C #>     A -0.13 -0.05 -0.03 #>     B -0.01 -0.08 -0.01 #>     C  0.06  0.02  0.04 #>  #>   Contemporaneous [undirected] #>     weights [0.006, 0.085]  |  +3 / -0 edges #>          A    B    C #>     A 0.00 0.06 0.08 #>     B 0.06 0.00 0.01 #>     C 0.08 0.01 0.00 #>  #>   Between [undirected] #>     no non-zero edges #>       A B C #>     A 0 0 0 #>     B 0 0 0 #>     C 0 0 0 #>  #>   plot(x) | plot(x, layer = "temporal") | plot(x, layer = "between")  #>   edges(x) | nodes(x) | summary(x) | coefs(x) | matrices(x) summary(fit) #>           network n_nodes n_edges density mean_abs_weight n_positive n_negative #> 1        temporal       3       6       1      0.03140474          2          4 #> 2 contemporaneous       3       3       1      0.05004674          3          0 #> 3         between       3       0       0      0.00000000          0          0 # }

title: Register an idiographic estimator or workflow — register_estimator
text: name Unique canonical method name. fit function, single character string naming function. aliases Optional alternative method names. kind Either "estimator" "workflow". description Short human-readable description. result_class Optional result classes used infer equivalence metadata objects produced direct calls estimator. equivalence named list describing validation status, reference, scope, tolerance, notes. Missing fields receive conservative defaults. overwrite Logical. Replace existing registration canonical name. Aliases owned another method never overwritten.

title: Register an idiographic estimator or workflow — register_estimator
code: demo_fitter <- function(data, ...) structure(list(data = data),                                              class = "demo_result") register_estimator("demo", demo_fitter, result_class = "demo_result") get_estimator("demo") #> function (data, ...)  #> structure(list(data = data), class = "demo_result") #> <environment: 0x56076f3fffe8> remove_estimator("demo")

title: Summary method for net_mlvar — summary.net_mlvar
code: # \donttest{ set.seed(1) n_id <- 8; n_t <- 30; vars <- c("A", "B", "C") rows <- lapply(seq_len(n_id), function(i) {   m <- as.data.frame(matrix(rnorm(n_t * 3), ncol = 3))   names(m) <- vars   m$id <- i; m$day <- 1L; m$beep <- seq_len(n_t)   m }) d <- do.call(rbind, rows) fit <- fit_mlvar(d, vars = vars, id = "id", day = "day", beep = "beep") #> Warning: Model for 'A': singular fit (random-effects variance near zero). #> Warning: Model for 'A': boundary (singular) fit: see help('isSingular') #> Warning: Model for 'B': singular fit (random-effects variance near zero). #> Warning: Model for 'B': boundary (singular) fit: see help('isSingular') #> Warning: Model for 'C': singular fit (random-effects variance near zero). #> Warning: Model for 'C': boundary (singular) fit: see help('isSingular') #> Warning: Between-subjects network not estimable: a random-intercept SD is 0 (no between-person variance). Returning a zero matrix by convention (mlVAR returns NA here). print(fit) #> mlVAR result: 8 subjects, 232 observations, 3 variables (lags 1) #>   Temporal edges significant at p<0.05: 1 / 9 #>  #>   Temporal [directed] #>     weights [-0.131, 0.062]  |  +3 / -6 edges #>           A     B     C #>     A -0.13 -0.05 -0.03 #>     B -0.01 -0.08 -0.01 #>     C  0.06  0.02  0.04 #>  #>   Contemporaneous [undirected] #>     weights [0.006, 0.085]  |  +3 / -0 edges #>          A    B    C #>     A 0.00 0.06 0.08 #>     B 0.06 0.00 0.01 #>     C 0.08 0.01 0.00 #>  #>   Between [undirected] #>     no non-zero edges #>       A B C #>     A 0 0 0 #>     B 0 0 0 #>     C 0 0 0 #>  #>   plot(x) | plot(x, layer = "temporal") | plot(x, layer = "between")  #>   edges(x) | nodes(x) | summary(x) | coefs(x) | matrices(x) summary(fit) #>           network n_nodes n_edges density mean_abs_weight n_positive n_negative #> 1        temporal       3       6       1      0.03140474          2          4 #> 2 contemporaneous       3       3       1      0.05004674          3          0 #> 3         between       3       0       0      0.00000000          0          0 # }

title: Validate one-step forecasts from idiographic VAR models (experimental) — validate_forecast
text: Experimental. rolling-origin design follows standard time-series cross-validation practice, unlike estimators package external reference implementation validate , interface, defaults, reported metrics may change future release. Performs rolling-origin one-step prediction fit_var() fit_graphical_var(). split fits estimator earlier blocks predicts current variables next block lag-1 values. Scaling within-person centring parameters learned training split , applied assessment split prediction.

title: Validate one-step forecasts from idiographic VAR models (experimental) — validate_forecast
code: validate_forecast(   data,   vars,   estimator = c("var", "graphical_var"),   id = NULL,   day = NULL,   beep = NULL,   initial = NULL,   assess = 1L,   step = 1L,   n_splits = NULL,   block_size = NULL,   scale = TRUE,   center_within = TRUE,   delete_missings = TRUE,   keep_fits = FALSE,   ... )

title: Validate one-step forecasts from idiographic VAR models (experimental) — validate_forecast
text: data data.frame matrix columns variables optional id/day/beep columns. vars Character vector variable names. estimator "var" (default) fit_var() "graphical_var" fit_graphical_var(). id Character. Name person-ID column, NULL. day Character. Name day/session column, NULL. beep Character. Name measurement-occasion column, NULL. initial Integer number ordered blocks first training split. Default uses 60 percent blocks, leaving least one assessment block. assess Integer number blocks assess per split. Default 1. step Integer number blocks advance splits. Default 1. n_splits Optional maximum number rolling splits. block_size Integer NULL. Consecutive block length used neither id day supplied. Defaults floor(sqrt(nrow(data))). scale Logical. Whether standardize using training-split means SDs. Default TRUE. center_within Logical. Whether centre within person using training-split person means one id present. Default TRUE. delete_missings Logical. Drop incomplete current/lagged assessment rows. Default TRUE. keep_fits Logical. Store fitted split models? Default FALSE. ... arguments passed estimator.

title: Validate one-step forecasts from idiographic VAR models (experimental) — validate_forecast
text: forecast_result $predictions, $metrics, $splits, $failures, optionally $fits.

title: Validate one-step forecasts from idiographic VAR models (experimental) — validate_forecast
code: set.seed(1) d <- data.frame(id = 1, day = rep(1:5, each = 12),                 beep = rep(1:12, 5),                 A = rnorm(60), B = rnorm(60), C = rnorm(60)) fc <- validate_forecast(d, vars = c("A", "B", "C"), id = "id",                         day = "day", beep = "beep",                         initial = 3, n_splits = 2, scale = FALSE) fc$metrics #>   variable  n       mae      rmse       bias #> 1        A 22 0.6627637 0.7916495  0.2286442 #> 2        B 22 0.7710222 0.8981746 -0.1662394 #> 3        C 22 1.0826584 1.3570916  0.8713974 #> 4 .overall 66 0.8388148 1.0448483  0.3112674

title: idiographic 0.3.2
text: New package Title — “Idiographic Person-Specific Heterogeneous Complex Networks” — rewritten Description method references doi:10.1007/978-3-031-95365-1_20 doi:10.1080/00273171.2018.1454823. Added Sonsoles López-Pernas package author. Documentation language standardized British English (Language: en-GB), dialect fixes across documentation prose new inst/WORDLIST package spell check runs clean. Slimmer CRAN footprint: competitor-equivalence tests now live repository’s opt-validation lane excluded CRAN tarball. Suggests trimmed 16 8 packages — removed gimme, graphicalVAR, glasso, corpcor, data.table, qgraph, rio, jsonlite, none shipped package uses.

title: idiographic 0.2.0
text: Made CRAN package offline-first: mandatory imports standard R packages, lme4, lavaan, plotting, external backends optional. Competitor-oracle tests real-panel corpus now run separate opt-validation/ lane excluded CRAN tarball. Added registry-backed fit_idiographic() front door, estimator discovery, method-specific equivalence() declarations, package-wide equivalence_table() argument--argument argument_coverage() ledgers, common tidy accessors. 17 registered methods 315 current public formals now executable evidence classification; new unassessed arguments fail closure test. Expanded direct-oracle testing across graphicalVAR option combinations, mlVAR multi-lag/preprocessing/unique-model configurations, bivariate plus three-variable GIMME standard, hybrid, VAR searches. GIMME evidence now also covers fit statistics, uneven panels, exogenous-variable dimensions, interacting correction/standardization controls. Tightened public argument validation engine-specific controls silently ignored. Closed remaining executable evidence cells: 12 supported lag-1 lmer mlVAR structure combinations, per-subject/missing-data graphicalVAR fits, GIMME 10.0 correction/stopping/standardization/cutoff/forced-path controls, standardized ML/MLR uSEM fits, Mplus wrapper forwarding/conversion, Bayesian burn-/thinning, positive random-residual recovery, parallel mlVAR, base-R linear/logistic idiographic-ML engine equality. Migrated 20-panel real ESM mlVAR validation corpus Dynalytics/psychaj work CRAN-excluded validation/ lane, self-contained raw inputs, mlVAR 0.7.3 frozen oracles, provenance hashes, explicit regression coverage missing IDs, irregular occasion gaps, degenerate -person networks. Duplicate observation keys now fail clearly instead producing row-order-dependent preprocessing. Uniform fit_* naming estimators (breaking). Every model-fitting verb now uses single fit_ prefix: fit_var(), fit_graphical_var(), fit_mlvar(), fit_rolling_var(), estimators. Short model nicknames passed compare_idiographic(), estimate_stability(), validate_forecast() (example, "var" "graphical_var") unchanged. New native Bayesian estimators statistically reproduce Mplus DSEM output without requiring Mplus: fit_mlvar_bayes() — two-level Bayesian VAR(1) latent mean centring. temporal = "fixed" statistically validated frozen Mplus DSEM fixed-temporal + random-intercept fixtures; temporal = "random" fits full DSEM person-specific temporal matrices random-effect covariance (reports random-slope SDs). fit_var_bayes() — single-level Bayesian VAR(1), unregularized Bayesian analogue fit_graphical_var(). Pure-R conjugate Gibbs sampler (hand-rolled inverse-Wishart draws; new dependencies). Posterior median / SD / 95% CI / one-tailed p, three networks (temporal, contemporaneous, ), Gelman-Rubin PSR diagnostic. Validated statistical (Monte-Carlo-error) equivalence real Mplus 9 output frozen ground-truth fixtures parity tests. Added fit_ml() idiographic supervised machine-learning: ordered within-person train/test splits, person-specific models, pooled baselines held-rows, regression/classification metrics, row-level predictions, coefficient extraction via coefs(). model names statistical/ML model (example, "ridge"), estimator names implementation/backend (default "native"). new dependencies: native models include mean/majority baselines, OLS/logistic, ridge, lasso, elastic net, PCR, LDA, Gaussian naive Bayes, kNN, one-split trees. fit_idiographic_ml() fit_individualized_ml() remain aliases.

title: idiographic 0.1.0
text: Initial CRAN submission. Provides idiographic network estimators intensive longitudinal data, including ordinary VAR, graphical VAR, mlVAR, uSEM, GIMME-style models. Includes preprocessing audits, rolling-window estimation, forecast validation, edge stability diagnostics, model comparison, tidy accessors, cograph plotting support.
